data.title: Vietnam, Philippines Raised to Upper-Middle Income Status
data.description: The World Bank raised Vietnam and the Philippines to upper-middle income status after years of brisk economic expansion, potentially boosting investor confidence in both countries, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Offers 5% Equity Stake to Trump Administration, Report Says
data.description:
OpenAI has proposed providing a 5% equity stake to the Trump administration, the Financial Times reported.

According to Jin10, the report did not provide additional details on the terms or timing of the proposal.

data.title: QDII Approved Quota Totals $176.17 Billion, SAFE Data Show
data.description:
China’s cumulative approved investment quota under the Qualified Domestic Institutional Investor (QDII) program totaled $176.17 billion as of the end of June, according to the latest disclosure from the State Administration of Foreign Exchange.

According to Jin10, the QDII quota approval table showed that securities and fund institutions accounted for $97.28 billion of the cumulative approved quota.

The data also indicated that the most recent QDII quota allocation was issued on March 23.

data.title: Japan 10-Year Bond Sale Draws Weaker Demand Than 12-Month Average
data.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond auction drew weaker demand than the average for the past year amid concerns the Bank of Japan isn’t raising rates quickly enough to contain inflation, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Citi’s Luet Says China Franchise Has Grown Strongly
data.description:
Marc Luet, Citi’s head of Japan, Asia North & Australia and banking, said the bank has seen the role of the yuan grow and is hopeful of obtaining a securities license in China, according to Bloomberg.

Luet made the comments in an exclusive interview on “Bloomberg: The China Show.”

data.title: Binance to Suspend Injective Network Deposits and Withdrawals for INJ Upgrade and Hard Fork
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the exchange will suspend deposits and withdrawals of token(s) on the Injective (INJ) network starting at approximately 2026-07-02 13:00 (UTC) to support a planned network upgrade and hard fork. Binance said the suspension is intended to facilitate the upgrade process and that it will manage the technical requirements involved for users during the event. The network upgrade and hard fork are scheduled to take place at the block height of 172,502,000, or approximately at 2026-07-02 14:00 (UTC). Binance added that trading of token(s) on the Injective network will not be impacted during the deposit and withdrawal pause.

Service Status and Resumption: Binance said deposits and withdrawals for token(s) on the Injective (INJ) network will be reopened once the upgraded network is deemed stable. The exchange stated that no further announcement will be posted regarding the reopening. Upgrade Timing Details: The deposit and withdrawal suspension is set to begin at approximately 2026-07-02 13:00 (UTC), ahead of the upgrade and hard fork expected around 2026-07-02 14:00 (UTC) at block height 172,502,000. Trading Impact: Binance said trading on the aforementioned network will not be affected while deposits and withdrawals are suspended.

data.title: Kosdaq Tech-Track Firms Face Delisting Review After Major Business Changes Within Five Years
data.description:
KRX has amended its listing rules so Kosdaq tech-track companies that change their main business within five years will be subject to a substantive delisting review.

According to NS3.AI, the revised rule is intended to address cases where listed firms pivot into new activities, including managing virtual asset treasuries.

The amendment excludes business changes that are considered similar to, or ancillary to, a company’s existing operations.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Hong Kong-Listed Gold Stocks Extend Gains, China Gold International Up Over 13%
data.description:
Hong Kong-listed gold stocks continued to strengthen, led by China Gold International (02099.HK), which rose more than 13%.

According to Jin10, Lingbao Gold (03330.HK) gained more than 11%, while Chifeng Gold (06693.HK) and Zijin Gold International (02259.HK) also moved higher.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Musk Says Optimus Mass Production Will Start Extremely Slowly
data.description:
Tesla CEO Elon Musk said the company’s Optimus humanoid robot would begin mass production at an “extremely slow” pace because the underlying technology is newly developed.

According to Jin10, Musk made the comment after posting on social media a photo of himself at an Optimus production line at Tesla’s Fremont factory in California, prompting renewed discussion about the robot’s manufacturing progress.

A social media user suggested Tesla had recently reduced public demonstrations of Optimus, possibly because mass production was advancing faster than market expectations. Musk rejected that interpretation, saying the initial production phase would be very slow and emphasizing that building Optimus is not comparable to manufacturing cars.

data.title: Bitcoin Fell 20.5% in June to $58,526 as PlanB Says It Could Drop to $52,000
data.description:
Bitcoin fell 20.5% in June, closing at $58,526 and ending the month below its 200-week moving average, while remaining above its realized price. According to NS3.AI, the move has renewed attention on whether Bitcoin could revisit levels seen in prior bear market lows.

Analyst PlanB said previous bear market bottoms occurred below Bitcoin’s realized price. Based on that comparison, PlanB said Bitcoin could drop to $52,000.

data.title: Supabase Reports Capacity Limits Affecting Project Operations in Most Regions
data.description:
Supabase said it is continuing to face capacity limits in most regions, affecting several project-related operations.

According to Foresight News, the PostgreSQL development platform said the constraints are impacting project creation, project scaling, project restarts, branch configuration, and restore operations.

Supabase said some users may experience repeated failures when attempting these actions. The company said its team is taking steps to increase capacity to restore normal service and will continue to provide updates on progress.

data.title: Vietnam Central Bank Warns Inflation Pressures to Stay Elevated, Moves to Stabilize Dong
data.description: The State Bank of Vietnam warned that inflationary pressures are likely to remain elevated throughout the year and said it has taken steps to ensure liquidity in the banking system and stabilize the dong amid global economic uncertainty, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | China A-Shares Slide at Midday as Precious Metals Rally
data.description:
China’s A-share market fell by midday after opening lower across major indexes, while the precious metals sector rose against the broader decline.

According to Jin10, the three main A-share indexes opened lower in the morning session, and the ChiNext Index briefly sank more than 4% early in trading before the market rebounded in choppy moves.

By the midday close, the Shanghai Composite Index was down 0.9%, the Shenzhen Component Index fell 2.09%, and the ChiNext Index dropped 3.47%. Turnover across the two markets exceeded 2.2 trillion yuan. More than 2,000 stocks declined.

Sector performance was mixed. Precious metals stocks surged, with Xianglu Tungsten, Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining, Zijin Mining Group’s Zhaojin Mining unit, and Shandong Gold International hitting their daily limit-up levels. The humanoid robot theme was active, with more than 10 constituent stocks also reaching limit-up.

In other areas, the advanced packaging theme continued to recover during the session. Semiconductor equipment stocks remained under pressure, while computing power hardware names fell broadly. Glass substrates and memory chip-related sectors were among the biggest decliners.

data.title: Oxmiq Raises $35 Million Led by Samsung Catalyst Fund and Fudomo
data.description:
AI chip startup Oxmiq said it has raised $35 million in a new funding round led by Samsung Catalyst Fund and Fudomo, with participation from Taiwan’s MediaTek and Pegatron Venture Capital, bringing its total funding to $60 million. According to ChainCatcher, the company was founded by former Intel chief architect Raja Koduri and is headquartered in Campbell, California.

Oxmiq said it plans to integrate GPU, CPU, and tensor engine components into a single licensable IP module, and to develop an integrated computing architecture that includes chiplets and memory. The company also said it is targeting the custom chip market and intends to compete with Broadcom, Marvell, and MediaTek.

Koduri said Oxmiq aims to become “the ARM of the next era.” The company said the new capital will be used to complete development of its first IP and bring it to market, and to expand its engineering team.

data.title: Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan Says STRC Volatility Signals Late-Cycle Conditions as Market Nears Bottom
data.description:
Bitwise Chief Investment Officer Matt Hougan said STRC’s volatility is a typical feature of the late stage of a crypto cycle and that the market is approaching a bottom. According to PANews, Hougan said STRC effectively allocates Bitcoin using capital that expects high returns and low volatility, while Bitcoin does not have those characteristics.

Hougan said this mismatch needs to be cleared out of the system before a bottom can form.

He also said Strategy’s role in the Bitcoin market has shifted from a one-way buyer to a participant that can trade more flexibly depending on market conditions.

Hougan added that institutional investors are expected to take a larger role, saying global banks, pension funds, and sovereign wealth funds are gradually entering the market. He cited signs including Morgan Stanley launching a proprietary Bitcoin ETF and Wells Fargo adding Bitcoin to model investment portfolios.

data.title: CATL Says Mining, Not Refining, Is Battery Making’s Big Hurdle
data.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. said mining, rather than refining, is the biggest hurdle in making batteries, as securing supplies of key raw materials becomes a priority for the world’s largest electric-vehicle battery maker, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Sliding Yen, Strong Economy Bolster BOJ Case for Earlier Rate Hike
data.description: The Bank of Japan has a stronger case to consider an early interest-rate hike as business activity remains robust and the tumbling yen threatens to push inflation above its price target, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: France Reports 77 Crypto-Linked Kidnapping and Extortion Cases So Far This Year
data.description:
France has recorded 77 cases so far this year involving crypto-linked kidnapping, unlawful detention, extortion, or attempted crimes, French Interior Minister Laurent Nunez said.

According to NS3.AI, Nunez said the total is up from 45 in 2025.

He added that about 200 people were arrested following incidents or during preventive operations.

data.title: Mango Network to Unlock 286.87 Million MGO Tokens on July 9
data.description:
Mango Network is scheduled to unlock about 286.87 million MGO tokens at 9:00 AM (UTC+8) on July 9.

According to ChainCatcher, the information comes from token unlock data tracked by Web3 asset data platform RootData, which estimates the unlocked tokens are worth about $2.85 million.

data.title: STOCKS | BOE A Shares Rebound Into Positive Territory After Hitting Intraday Limit-Down
data.description:
BOE A shares rose before midday to trade in positive territory after earlier touching the intraday limit-down price.

According to Jin10, the stock had briefly hit its limit-down level during the morning session before reversing higher.
